creator: michaelo@FreeBSD.org
text:
OK, I think I have found at least the issue why it was failing for me and maybe for you. It seems to be a poudriere issue:
> # poudriere testport -S -j 133-release-amd64 -p default-head -z cafe -O dw-custom -b latest -i math/py-pandas
> [00:00:00] Creating the reference jail... done
> [00:00:01] Mounting system devices for 133-release-amd64-default-head-cafe
> [00:00:01] Stashing existing package repository
> [00:00:01] Mounting ports from: /var/poudriere/ports/default-head
> [00:00:01] Mounting ports overlay from: /var/poudriere/ports/dw-custom
> [00:00:01] Mounting packages from: /var/poudriere/data/packages/133-release-amd64-default-head-cafe
> [00:00:01] Mounting distfiles from: /var/poudriere/data/distfiles
> [00:00:01] Copying /var/db/ports from: /usr/local/etc/poudriere.d/133-release-amd64-default-head-cafe-options
> [00:00:01] Appending to make.conf: /usr/local/etc/poudriere.d/133-release-amd64-default-head-make.conf
> [00:00:01] Appending to make.conf: /usr/local/etc/poudriere.d/133-release-amd64-default-head-cafe-make.conf
> /etc/resolv.conf -> /var/poudriere/data/.m/133-release-amd64-default-head-cafe/ref/etc/resolv.conf
> [00:00:01] Starting jail 133-release-amd64-default-head-cafe
> Updating /var/run/os-release done.
> [00:00:01] Will build as nobody:nobody (65534:65534)
> [00:00:02] Ports supports: FLAVORS SUBPACKAGES SELECTED_OPTIONS
> [00:00:02] Inspecting /var/poudriere/data/.m/133-release-amd64-default-head-cafe/ref//usr/ports for modifications to git checkout... yes
> [00:00:03] Ports top-level git hash: a5fc087131e6 (dirty)
> [00:00:03] Acquiring build logs lock for 133-release-amd64-default-head-cafe... done
> [00:00:03] Logs: /var/poudriere/data/logs/bulk/133-release-amd64-default-head-cafe/2024-12-10T11:32:29
> [00:00:03] Loading MOVED for /var/poudriere/data/.m/133-release-amd64-default-head-cafe/ref/usr/ports
> [00:00:03] Gathering ports metadata
> [00:00:04] Calculating ports order and dependencies
> [00:00:05] Sanity checking the repository
> [00:00:05] Trimming IGNORED and blacklisted ports
> [00:00:05] Package fetch: Looking for missing packages to fetch from pkg+http://pkg.FreeBSD.org/${ABI}/latest
> Updating FreeBSD repository catalogue...
> [133-release-amd64-default-head-cafe] Fetching meta.conf: 100%    178 B   0.2kB/s    00:01
> [133-release-amd64-default-head-cafe] Fetching data.pkg: 100%    7 MiB   7.7MB/s    00:01
> Processing entries: 100%
> FreeBSD repository update completed. 35797 packages processed.
> All repositories are up to date.
> [00:00:25] Package fetch: Will fetch 1 packages from remote or local pkg cache
> 
> No packages are required to be fetched.
> Integrity check was successful.
> [00:00:25] Package fetch: Using cached copy of py310-cython-0.29.37_1
> [00:00:25] Checking packages for incremental rebuild needs
> [00:00:25] Skipping recursive rebuild

The canonical default version of Python is 3.11, but I set 3.10 in my poudriere build. As you can see poudriere has been instructed to use upstream packages as much as possible, but it does not know that the cython package it is downloading has been built as a non-default version, thus "$PREFIX/bin/cython" is not present in that package and fails py-pandas. In theory poudriere should detect that, build such packages locally and then "$PREFIX/bin/cython" AND "$PREFIX/bin/cython-3.10" would be there.

Bryan, should I report this as a poudriere bug?
